name: Water Route — from Taramundi to As Veigas and back
name_original: Ruta del Agua — de Taramundi a As Veigas y vuelta
region_intro: Hiking around Taramundi offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, lush green forests, and numerous rivers and streams. The region is part of the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve, known for its rich biodiversity and water-rich environment. Hikers can explore deep valleys, discover waterfalls, and traverse trails that highlight the area's natural beauty and cultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Taramundi The most popular hiking route is Water Route — from Taramundi to As Veigas and back, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route explores the historical significance of water use, passing traditional villages and a waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is A Salgueira Waterfall – Castro de Taramundi loop from Taramundi, an easy 3.4 miles (5.5 km) path. This circular route leads to the Sarguera waterfall and passes by the Taramundi Hillfort. Local hikers also love the Castro de Taramundi – Taramundi Knife Museum loop from Taramundi, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through cultural landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Hiking around Taramundi is defined by its water-rich environment, lush forests, and historical sites like hillforts and traditional mills. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Taramundi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Taramundi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Taramundi?
A: Taramundi offers a diverse network of over 14 hiking trails. These routes allow you to explore the region's unique landscapes, from lush forests to historic villages and water-powered mills.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Taramundi?
A: The trails in Taramundi cater to various abilities, with 5 easy routes and 9 moderate routes. There are no difficult trails, making the area accessible for most hikers.

Q: What makes hiking in Taramundi unique?
A: Hiking in Taramundi is characterized by its water-rich environment, with numerous rivers, streams, and waterfalls. The trails often weave through lush forests and connect traditional villages, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage, including ancient hillforts and historic mills.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Taramundi?
A: Yes, Taramundi features several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall – Castro de Taramundi loop from Taramundi&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.4-mile (5.5 km) path that takes you to the Sarguera waterfall and past the Taramundi Hillfort. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Taramundi – Taramundi Knife Museum loop from Taramundi&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.8-mile (6.1 km) trail connecting cultural landmarks.

Q: Can I find hikes that feature waterfalls in Taramundi?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall – Castro de Taramundi loop from Taramundi&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many other routes, like the 'Ruta del Agua', also highlight the area's abundant water sources.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Taramundi?
A: Yes, Taramundi offers several easy and moderate trails suitable for families. The shorter circular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Salgueira Waterfall – Castro de Taramundi loop&lt;/a&gt;, are great for an outing with children, combining natural sights with historical points of interest.

Q: What cultural or historical sites can I explore while hiking in Taramundi?
A: Taramundi's trails are rich in cultural and historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazonovo Mill Museum&lt;/a&gt;, explore the traditional village of As Veigas, or see the ancient Taramundi Hillfort. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Taramundi – Taramundi Knife Museum loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to combine these cultural landmarks.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Taramundi?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's stunning natural beauty, the tranquility of its forests and rivers, and the unique blend of outdoor activity with cultural exploration of its traditional villages and historical sites.

Q: Are there any specific routes that showcase the region's traditional water mills?
A: Yes, the 'Ruta de los Molinos' (Mills Route) is specifically designed to highlight the area's characteristic water mills, offering insight into a long-standing tradition. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazonovo Mill Museum&lt;/a&gt;, which houses the largest collection of mills in Spain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Taramundi?
A: Taramundi's lush environment makes it appealing throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with spring bringing blooming flora and autumn showcasing rich fall colors. Summer is also popular, though trails can be busier. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions to visit after a hike in Taramundi?
A: Beyond the trails, Taramundi offers several attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4744403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazonovo Mill Museum&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Martín&lt;/a&gt;, or see the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Vilaoudriz&lt;/a&gt;. The village of As Veigas, part of the Oscos-Eo Biosphere Reserve, is also a charming place to explore.

Q: Is Taramundi part of a larger natural reserve?
A: Yes, Taramundi is located within the Eo, Oscos, and Buron lands Biosphere Reserve. This designation highlights the region's rich biodiversity and significant flora and fauna, ensuring a well-preserved natural environment for hikers to enjoy.
tips: The museum is dedicated to the traditional water mills that used to be used in the area to grind corn and other cereals. Visitors to the museum can explore the various mills and learn about their history and operation. The museum also offers guided tours and demonstrations of how the mills were used in the past. It is a unique and fascinating place to visit, offering insight into the region's cultural heritage and traditional way of life.

The waterfall is formed by the Salgueira River, which flows through a deep gorge before falling over a rocky ledge and cascading down into a pool below. The height of the waterfall is approximately 30 meters (98 feet). What you want is to stay in silence for a while contemplating this wonderful waterfall in a beautiful setting
As Veigas is the prototype of traditional Asturian architecture in a great state of conservation.

The Teixois Ethnographic Complex has been declared a site of cultural interest.
It is a small village where you can discover a valuable set of hydraulic devices in their natural state.
